data.title: SpaceX Tells Staff It Is Preparing for a Potential IPO Next Year — WSJ
data.description:
By Micah Maidenberg

SpaceX told employees it is preparing for a possible public offering next year, confirming a potential listing for a company that has built formidable rocket and satellite businesses since Elon Musk founded it in 2002.

"The thinking is that if we execute brilliantly and the markets cooperate, a public offering could raise a significant amount of capital, " Bret Johnsen, the company's finance chief, wrote Friday in a message to SpaceX staff.

Johnsen said that whether a listing would happen and when it might take place were "highly uncertain," according to the message, a copy of which was reviewed by The Wall Street Journal. He didn't discuss a valuation the company could target in an IPO.

SpaceX didn't immediately respond to requests for comment. The Journal and other news outlets reported the Texas-based company was weighing a public offering in 2026.

Any IPO proceeds could be used to ramp up launches of the company's Starship rocket to "an insane flight rate," deploy artificial-intelligence data centers in space, and work on uncrewed and human missions to Mars, Johnsen wrote.

Texas-based SpaceX is the world's busiest rocket launcher, frequently using its fleet of Falcon 9 vehicles to take customer satellites and its own Starlink satellites to low-Earth orbit.

Starlink, which uses more than 9,000 satellites to provide high-speed internet connections, has grown quickly in recent years, helping drive the company's valuation to $400 billion earlier this year.

That valuation is set to jump again, reaching around $800 billion based on a new stock price of $421 a share that Johnsen disclosed in his memo Friday, people familiar with the company said. SpaceX typically offers employees and former staffers two opportunities each year to sell some of their shareholdings in the private company.

Write to Micah Maidenberg at micah.maidenberg@wsj.com

data.title: Key facts: SEAT welcomes EU's potential reversal of 2035 ban; VW faces £11B FCA compensation
data.description:
• Volkswagen's subsidiary SEAT noted that the European Commission's likely reversal of the 2035 combustion-engine ban would aid its transition to electric vehicles amid slow customer adoption.1
  • Volkswagen's finance unit is included in a UK FCA plan to compensate for mis-sold car loans, costing lenders around 11 billion pounds for loans issued over 17 years until 2024.2

data.title: Key facts: Rheinmetall secures Dutch Skyranger 30 contract; partners with Hensoldt
data.description:
• Rheinmetall has won a contract with the Dutch Defense for multiple Skyranger 30 air defense systems, worth hundreds of millions of euros, with deliveries set for late 2028 to 2029.1
  • Rheinmetall's air defense unit has signed a deal with Hensoldt to supply SPEXER radars for Skyranger 30 systems and HoWiSM drone defense, effective until the 2030s.2

data.title: Key facts: Novo Nordisk's Wegovy® approved by EMA; Ozempic launched in India; Mike Doustdar named CEO
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk's Wegovy® 7.2 mg received a positive opinion from the European Medicines Agency, showing 20.7% average weight loss in trials. U.S. and UK reviews are ongoing.12
  • Novo Nordisk launched its diabetes drug Ozempic in India, priced at $24 weekly, far lower than the U.S. price of $249.40. It offers three dosage options.345
  • Novo Nordisk has appointed Mike Doustdar as CEO and reshaped its board under Lars Rebien Sorensen, preparing for product launches and talks with Hims & Hers Health for telehealth services.6

data.title: Key facts: Investor Interest Shifts to Airlines; Lufthansa Boosts Flights
data.description:
• Kepler Cheuvreux noted that investor interest is shifting towards airlines with significant exposure to premium, international, and long-haul travel, which may enhance Lufthansa's risk/reward profile.1
  • In 2025, flights from Europe to Nashville have nearly doubled year-over-year, reaching 665, with Lufthansa contributing to the increase in air connectivity and tourist arrivals.2

data.title: Key facts: Euronext to Launch Evening Trading for Futures; CAC40 Changes
data.description:
• Euronext will add a two-hour evening trading session for milling wheat, rapeseed, and maize futures starting February 2, from 6:30 p.m. to 8:30 p.m. CET.1
  • Euronext has made changes to the CAC40 index by adding Eiffage and excluding Edenred.2

data.title: Key facts: Deutsche Bank forecasts 2% policy rate; reports trades; funds solar project
data.description:
• Deutsche Bank has projected a terminal policy rate of 2%, with expectations for the next rate hike to occur in mid-2027, based on anticipated fiscal easing and a tight labor market.1
  • On December 12, 2025, Deutsche Bank AG reported transactions under Rule 8.5, including trades by an exempt principal trader for Bluefield Solar Inc. and Greencore Group PLC.2
  • In January 2024, Deutsche Bank participated in a syndicate of lenders that provided a A$160 million (US$106.77 million) project finance loan for the Munna Creek solar farm in Australia.3

data.title: Key facts: Brazil's Audit Court advises against Maersk in Santos auction; Maersk names Robert Erni as CFO
data.description:
• Brazil's Federal Audit Court has advised excluding Maersk and other terminal operators from the upcoming auction for the Tecon 10 terminal at Santos port due to market concentration concerns.1
  • Maersk appointed Robert Erni as CFO, replacing Patrick Jany, who will leave in early 2026. Jany will manage the year-end closing and annual report due February 5.23

data.title: Key facts: BNP Paribas in talks to sell BMCI stake; faces new tax impacts; reveals JTC PLC position
data.description:
• BNP Paribas is in talks to sell its 67% stake in Moroccan subsidiary BMCI to Holmarcom Group. The sale could boost BNP's CET1 ratio by about 15 basis points.123
  • BNP Paribas faces a new 5% tax on dividends and a rise in minority holding tax to 15% due to the India-France treaty, affecting capital gains taxation on equity shares.4
  • On December 12, 2025, BNP Paribas London revealed its public position on JTC PLC, outlining interests and short positions in relevant securities, as required by the Takeover Code.5

data.title: Bitcoin hoarding company Strategy remains in Nasdaq 100
data.description:
Bitcoin hoarding giant Strategy clung to its place in the Nasdaq 100 on Friday, continuing its year-long stint in the benchmark at a time where analysts have raised questions over its business model.

Some market watchers have suggested Strategy's pioneering business model of buying-and-holding bitcoin, which has spawned dozens of copycats, more closely resembles that of an investment fund.

Concerns have grown over the sustainability of crypto treasury companies, whose shares have proved extremely sensitive to bitcoin's gyrations.

Nasdaq said Biogen , CDW Corporation , Globalfoundries , Lululemon Athletica , On Semiconductor and Trade Desk were removed from the tech-heavy exchange's benchmark index.

New entrants included Alnylam Pharmaceuticals , Ferrovial , Insmed , Monolithic Power Systems , Seagate Technology and Western Digital .

Strategy started out as software company, MicroStrategy, but pivoted to bitcoin investing in 2020. It was included in the Nasdaq 100 last December under the index's technology sub-category.

Global index provider MSCI has also raised concerns about the presence of digital-asset treasury companies in its benchmarks. MSCI is due to decide in January whether to exclude Strategy and similar companies.

The Nasdaq changes are expected to take effect on December 22. The Nasdaq 100 index tracks the largest non-financial companies by market capitalization listed on the exchange.
